patch.value:
The hash-chained JSONL streams prove sequence integrity (tampering is detectable), and pm-3z0k makes that proof portable for third parties. But the author recorded on every entry is self-asserted: PM_AUTHOR is a plain environment variable, so any writer can record any identity. Integrity and authenticity are different guarantees.

For the horizon-4 visions this matters: organizations running business workflows on pm SDK primitives (pm-8ngt) with role/policy enforcement (pm-xc68) need 'who approved this transition' to be verifiable, not just recorded. Policy enforcement without author authenticity can be bypassed by claiming another principal's name.

Exploration scope (spike + ADR, parked P4 like pm-xc68):
- Optional per-entry signatures over the entry payload + chain anchor, so a signature also binds to chain position.
- Key model: per-author keypairs with a workspace trust store; investigate reusing git commit-signing infrastructure since the tracker is git-native.
- Verification: history --verify gains an authenticity dimension; unsigned entries in an opt-in workspace are flagged, mixed workspaces degrade gracefully.
- Cost: measure bytes-per-entry and verify-time overhead at pm-mi2x scale before committing.
- Explicit non-goal: authorization (what a principal MAY do) stays in pm-xc68; this story is only about proving what a principal DID record.

Layering: hash chain = tamper-evidence (shipped), pm-3z0k = portable proof, this = authenticity, pm-xc68 = authorization. Together they complete the recorded-immutable-proof invariant for multi-organization use.
As an organization running thousands of agents, I can verify which principal recorded each history entry: per-entry author signatures complementing hash-chain tamper-evidence

Spike + ADR only at this stage: a written exploration evaluates per-entry signature schemes (author keypairs, git-commit-signing reuse, workspace trust store), key distribution/rotation for thousands of agent principals, verify integration into the existing history --verify path, storage/token overhead per entry, and opt-in configuration; the ADR states an accept/defer decision with scale measurements from the pm-mi2x workspaces; no implementation lands before the ADR is accepted
